# Defend wage determination assumption (Editor Additional Comment 3)

## Summary
Editor asked: is "firm pays the demanded wage" (TIOLI) realistic on the platform? Could bargaining happen off-platform? This is a **realism / external-validity** concern — Peyman flagged "more substantial point, not me." **Coauthor task.** Probably resolved with a short institutional defense paragraph, no new analysis.

## Files
- Current theory setup (where the assumption lives): `writeup/hot_towel.tex` §3.1 Setup
- Empirical wages section: `writeup/hot_towel.tex` §5.4 Wages (lines 834–948)
- Editor letter: `referee_reports/Hot Towel - AEJ Micro Revision.docx` (Additional Comment 3)

## Keep in mind
- Peyman's explicit margin note on this item: **"More substantial point, not me."**
- GPT meeting memo suggests this probably doesn't need new analysis, just a sharper institutional defense.
- Also consider R2's suggestion (smaller comment, p. 34): if outside-option data exist, study surplus rather than wages. Probably minor.

## Actionable items
- [ ] Coauthors: write a short paragraph (≤ half a page) defending "firm pays the bid" as accurately reflecting platform rules.
- [ ] Coauthors: discuss known limits — off-platform bargaining, renegotiation, selection effects.
- [ ] Coauthors: decide whether to add a robustness discussion paragraph (probably yes, minimal) or leave as institutional defense only.
- [ ] Coauthors: decide whether to respond to R2 p. 34 suggestion about studying surplus vs. wages (depends on whether outside-option data is available).
- [ ] Peyman: once paragraph is drafted, integrate into the paper at the appropriate location (likely end of Setup or start of §5.4).

Editor's Letter Additional Comment 3; R2 smaller comments on p. 34.
